title: salix daiseniensis f. rotundifolia
intro: Salix daiseniensis f. rotundifolia, also known as the willow leaf or round-leaved willow, is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix daiseniensis f. rotundifolia include relief from menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate headaches. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark or leaves can be infused in hot water to make a tea, or it can be used in tinctures, salves, or poultices to treat various health issues.
